Title: App boots with security middleware order
Preconditions: Test runner can import app module
Test Steps: 1. Import src/app.ts; 2. Inspect Express use/disable calls; 3. Verify call order
Expected Results: x-powered-by disabled; helmet and json middleware registered before routers
Test Data: Input: module=app; Mock: express and helmet spies; Technique: Use Case Testing

Title: App mounts auth router under auth prefix
Test Steps: 1. Import src/app.ts; 2. Capture app.use registrations; 3. Assert auth router mounted on /auth
Expected Results: Auth routes mounted at /auth
Test Data: Input: routePrefix=/auth; Mock: authRouter object; Technique: Use Case Testing

Title: Health endpoint returns UP payload
Test Steps: 1. Resolve registered GET /health handler; 2. Invoke handler with mock response; 3. Inspect payload
Expected Results: Response JSON includes status=UP and ISO timestamp string
Test Data: Input: GET /health; Mock: response.json spy; Technique: Use Case Testing

Title: Index mounts job role router
Test Steps: 1. Import src/index.ts; 2. Inspect app.use calls; 3. Confirm jobRoleRouter mounted
Expected Results: Job role router attached to app
Test Data: Input: module=index; Mock: app.use spy; Technique: Use Case Testing

Title: Index uses default port when PORT missing
Test Steps: 1. Clear PORT; 2. Import src/index.ts; 3. Inspect listen arguments
Expected Results: Server listens on 3000
Test Data: Input: PORT=undefined; Mock: app.listen spy; Technique: Boundary Value Analysis

Title: Index uses numeric PORT when provided
Preconditions: PORT env var set to numeric value
Test Steps: 1. Set PORT=4001; 2. Import src/index.ts; 3. Inspect listen arguments
Expected Results: Server listens on configured numeric port
Test Data: Input: PORT=4001; Mock: app.listen spy; Technique: Use Case Testing

Title: Index falls back for non numeric PORT
Preconditions: PORT env var set to non numeric string
Test Steps: 1. Set PORT=abc; 2. Import src/index.ts; 3. Inspect listen arguments
Expected Results: Server falls back to port 3000
Test Data: Input: PORT=abc; Mock: app.listen spy; Technique: Decision Tables

Title: Index accepts zero PORT value
Test Steps: 1. Set PORT=0; 2. Import src/index.ts; 3. Inspect listen arguments
Expected Results: Server listens on port 0
Test Data: Input: PORT=0; Mock: app.listen spy; Technique: Boundary Value Analysis

Title: Index logs startup message with selected port
Test Steps: 1. Import src/index.ts; 2. Execute listen callback; 3. Assert console.log payload
Expected Results: Startup log includes effective port
Test Data: Input: PORT=4001; Mock: console.log spy; Technique: Use Case Testing

Title: Prisma client reuses singleton instance
Test Steps: 1. Call getPrismaClient twice; 2. Compare references
Expected Results: Second call returns same PrismaClient instance
Test Data: Env: DATABASE_URL=postgres URL; Technique: Use Case Testing

Title: Prisma client throws when DATABASE_URL missing
Test Steps: 1. Unset DATABASE_URL; 2. Call getPrismaClient; 3. Capture error
Expected Results: Throws required DATABASE_URL error
Test Data: Env: DATABASE_URL=; Technique: Risk Based Testing

Title: Seed database upserts capabilities bands and roles
Preconditions: Seed prisma stub with upsert methods
Test Steps: 1. Call seedDatabase(prismaStub); 2. Verify upsert invocation counts; 3. Inspect summary
Expected Results: Returns summary capabilities=3 bands=3 jobRoles=3
Test Data: Mock: capability.upsert; band.upsert; jobRole.upsert; Technique: Use Case Testing

Title: Seed database uses seeded relation ids in job role upserts
Preconditions: Capability and band upserts return deterministic ids
Test Steps: 1. Stub ids from capability and band upsert; 2. Call seedDatabase; 3. Inspect jobRole capabilityId and bandId
Expected Results: Job role records reference seeded ids correctly
Test Data: Mock IDs: capability=11,22,33; band=101,202,303; Technique: Equivalence Partitioning

Title: runSeed logs summary and disconnects on success
Preconditions: getPrismaClient mocked with successful seed behavior
Test Steps: 1. Call runSeed; 2. Inspect console.info and disconnect calls
Expected Results: Logs success summary and disconnects once; exitCode unchanged
Test Data: Mock: seedDatabase internal upserts succeed; Technique: Use Case Testing

Title: runSeed logs failures sets exitCode and disconnects
Test Steps: 1. Call runSeed with failing prisma stub; 2. Inspect console.error exitCode disconnect
Expected Results: Logs failure details; sets process.exitCode=1; disconnect still called
Test Data: Mock: jobRole.upsert throws Error(job role insert failed); Technique: Error Guessing

Title: Constants expose TTL defaults and auth prefix
Test Steps: 1. Import constants with env unset; 2. Assert ACCESS_TOKEN_TTL REFRESH_TOKEN_TTL AUTH_HEADER_PREFIX
Expected Results: Defaults are 15m and 7d and Bearer prefix constant
Test Data: Env: ACCESS_TOKEN_TTL unset; REFRESH_TOKEN_TTL unset; Technique: Boundary Value Analysis

Title: Constants trim custom TTL environment values
Test Steps: 1. Set ACCESS_TOKEN_TTL and REFRESH_TOKEN_TTL with spaces; 2. Import constants; 3. Assert trimmed values
Expected Results: TTL constants use trimmed env values
Test Data: Env: ACCESS_TOKEN_TTL=' 30m '; REFRESH_TOKEN_TTL=' 14d '; Technique: Equivalence Partitioning

Title: Express request typing supports optional authUser
Test Steps: 1. Reference Request.authUser in middleware/controller test fixture; 2. Run type-check
Expected Results: Type system accepts optional authUser with id email role shape
Test Data: Input: request.authUser assignment in TS test file; Technique: Use Case Testing

Title: Prisma seed enforces development-only auth user seeding
Test Steps: 1. Set ENABLE_DEV_TEST_USER=true and NODE_ENV=test; 2. Execute prisma/seed.ts; 3. Capture failure
Expected Results: Script throws safety error and exits with status 1
Test Data: Env: ENABLE_DEV_TEST_USER=true; NODE_ENV=test; Technique: Decision Tables

Title: Prisma seed skips auth-user seeding when feature flag disabled
Test Steps: 1. Execute prisma/seed.ts with development env and flag false; 2. Inspect calls to prisma.user.upsert
Expected Results: Job role data is seeded and no auth users are upserted
Test Data: Env: NODE_ENV=development; ENABLE_DEV_TEST_USER=false; Technique: Use Case Testing

Title: Prisma seed upserts user and admin when dev flag enabled
Test Steps: 1. Execute prisma/seed.ts with flag enabled; 2. Inspect prisma.user.upsert calls
Expected Results: Two upserts occur for user and admin with expected roles
Test Data: Env: NODE_ENV=development; ENABLE_DEV_TEST_USER=true; Technique: Decision Tables

Title: Prisma seed uses fallback TEST credentials when missing
Test Steps: 1. Execute prisma/seed.ts with TEST_* unset; 2. Inspect upsert payload emails
Expected Results: Defaults test@example.com and admin@example.com are used
Test Data: Env: TEST_USER_EMAIL unset; TEST_ADMIN_EMAIL unset; Technique: Equivalence Partitioning

Title: Prisma seed logs error disconnects and exits on failure
Preconditions: prisma operation fails during script execution
Test Steps: 1. Mock prisma upsert failure; 2. Run prisma/seed.ts main flow; 3. Observe disconnect and exit behavior
Expected Results: Error is logged; prisma disconnect is called; process exits with code 1
Test Data: Mock: capability.upsert throws; Technique: Error Guessing
